name: Luigi (Pipeline & Task Orchestration)

auth.notes: Local scheduler or central scheduler (luigid); no auth by default

performance.notes: Task graph build time; worker execution is parallelizable

agent_readiness.notes: Python pipeline framework. import luigi. class MyTask(luigi.Task): date = luigi.DateParameter(). def requires(self): return OtherTask(self.date). def output(self): return luigi.LocalTarget(f'data/{self.date}.csv'). def run(self): pass. luigi.build([MyTask(date=datetime.date.today())], workers=4). Target: LocalTarget, S3Target, GCSTarget. Dependency graph auto-resolved. Idempotent: output() guards re-runs. Central scheduler: luigid daemon with web UI :8082. Contrib: hadoop, spark, postgres, s3, gcs. @luigi.util.inherits for parameter inheritance.

gotchas: Requires output() to be defined — tasks without output are not idempotent
No built-in retry logic — manual implementation needed
Central scheduler (luigid) is a SPOF without HA setup
Slow to adopt modern Python patterns — no type annotations
Less active development vs Prefect/Dagster for modern pipelines
best_when: Batch data pipelines with clear dependency graphs and idempotency
Legacy pipeline systems already using Luigi
Simple ETL workflows without complex orchestration needs
When Prefect/Dagster is overkill for simple DAG execution
avoid_when: Real-time or streaming pipelines (use Apache Flink/Kafka)
Complex retry and alerting logic (use Prefect or Dagster)
Cloud-native workflows needing Kubernetes execution (use Flyte or Argo)
ML pipelines with experiment tracking (use Metaflow or ZenML)
summary: Luigi is Spotify's Python pipeline framework. Task subclass with requires(), output(), run(). luigi.build([Task()], workers=N). S3/GCS/Local targets. Dependency DAG auto-resolved. Idempotency via output() check. Web UI at :8082. Apache-2.0 by Spotify. Best for simple batch ETL with clear dependencies. No built-in retry. Less active than Prefect/Dagster for modern use. luigid scheduler is SPOF. Good for teams already using it.
